hint: Hold up 1 finger on each hand — count them all!
Hold up 1 finger, then 2 more — count them all!
Start at 2, count one more: 3!
Double 2! Two pairs make 4.
Start at 3, count up 2: 4, 5!
Start at 3, count up 2: 4, 5!
Start at 4, count one more: 5!
Start at 4, count one more: 5!
Double 3! Like two tricycles — 6 wheels!
Start at 5, count one more: 6!
Start at 5 (the bigger one), count up 1: 6!
Start at 5, count up 2: 6, 7!
Start at 5 (the bigger one), count up 2: 6, 7!
Start at 5, count up 3: 6, 7, 8!
Double 4! A spider has 8 legs — that's 4 + 4.
Two high-fives! 5 + 5 = 10.
8 + 2 = 10, then add 1 more = 11!
9 + 1 = 10, then add 3 more = 13!
Double 7! Two weeks make 14 days!
8 + 2 = 10, then add 3 more = 13!
6 and 4 are number bonds of 10!
7 and 3 make 10 — they're number bonds!
Double 6! Like two egg carton halves — 12 eggs!
9 + 1 = 10, then add 5 more = 15!
Start at 3, count back 1: 2!
4 take away 2 — double 2 is 4, so half is 2!
Start at 5, count back 1: 4!
Start at 5, count back 2: 4, 3!
5 is made of 3 and 2 — take away 3 and 2 is left!
Double 3 is 6, so 6 take away 3 is 3!
Start at 7, count back 2: 6, 5!
Double 4 is 8, so 8 take away 4 is 4!
Start at 9, count back 3: 8, 7, 6!
10 is made of 5 and 5 — take away 5 and 5 is left!
10 is made of 3 and 7 — take away 3 and 7 is left!
Start at 12, count back 2: 11, 10!
14 take away 4 lands right on 10!
15 take away 5 — hop back to 10!
Take the 7 away from 17 and you land on 10!
Start at 6, count back 2: 5, 4!
10 is made of 4 and 6 — take away 4 and 6 is left!
Take the 6 away from 16 and you land right on 10!
Start at 8, count back 3: 7, 6, 5!
20 is two tens — take one ten away and you have 10!

description: I have 3 sides and 3 corners.
hint: Count the sides — 1, 2, 3!

description: I am perfectly round with no corners at all.
hint: No corners, no sides — it rolls like a ball!

description: I have 4 equal sides and 4 square corners.
hint: All 4 sides are the same length — like a window!

description: I have 4 sides — 2 long ones and 2 short ones.
hint: Like a door — taller than it is wide.

description: I look like an egg — round but stretched out.
hint: Like a circle that got stretched longer!

description: I have 4 sides and stand on one point like a kite.
hint: A square tilted on its point!

description: I look like a slice of pizza!
hint: Count the edges of that pizza slice — 3!

description: I look like a wheel — perfectly round.
hint: A wheel is flat and round — no corners!

description: I have 3 sides and look like a mountain peak.
hint: Mountains have a pointy peak — 3 sides!

description: I look like a book cover — longer than I am wide.
hint: Two long sides and two short sides!

description: I look like a coin — flat and round.
hint: Coins are perfectly round!

description: I look like a tent — pointy on top, flat on the bottom.
hint: A tent has a peak — count the sides!

description: I look like a clock face — no corners anywhere.
hint: Clocks are round!

description: I have 4 equal sides but my corners are NOT square.
hint: Like a square leaning to the side — tilted!

description: I look like a ball — round all over!
hint: A ball is round in every direction — that's a sphere!

description: I look like a box with 6 flat square faces.
hint: A gift box has flat square sides — it's a cube!

description: I look like a tin can — round on top and bottom.
hint: A can has two circles and a curved side — that's a cylinder!

description: I look like an ice cream cone — pointy at the bottom!
hint: An ice cream cone has a circle on top and a point on the bottom!

description: I look like dice — all my sides are the same size.
hint: Dice are cubes — 6 equal square faces!

description: I look like a globe — round like the Earth!
hint: The Earth is shaped like a sphere — round everywhere!
Just counting up! What comes after 3?
Counting up by 1! What comes after 4?
Keep counting! What comes after 6?
Almost there! What comes after 9?
Counting by 2s! Skip one number each time!
Counting by 2s starting from 1! Odd numbers.
Counting down! 5, 4, 3, 2!
Counting backwards! Like a rocket launch countdown!
Each number appears twice! 1,1 then 2,2 then 3,?
It goes back and forth! 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, ?
Skip counting by 3! Add 3 each time.
Skip counting by 5! Add 5 each time.
Skip counting by 10! Add 10 each time.
Each number doubles! 1, 2, 4, 8!
Double it each time! 2, 4, 8, 16!
Skip counting by 3 again! Add 3 each time.
Counting by 5s! Keep going — 25!
Each number doubles! 3, 6, 12, 24!

question: What number comes AFTER 7?
hint: Count: 7, then the next one is 8!

question: What number comes BEFORE 5?
hint: Count up: 4 comes right before 5!

question: What number comes AFTER 9?
hint: After 9 comes a big number — 10!

question: What number comes BEFORE 10?
hint: Count: 9 comes right before 10!

question: What number comes AFTER 14?
hint: 14, then the next number is 15!

question: What number comes BETWEEN 6 and 8?
hint: 6, then 7, then 8 — 7 is in the middle!

question: What number comes AFTER 19?
hint: After 19 comes 20 — that's two tens!

question: What number comes BEFORE 1?
hint: Before 1 comes 0 — that means none!
3 is more than 1 — you can count higher!
8 is more than 5 — count up and you'll get further!
12 has more than 8 — it's past 10!
9 is closer to 10, so 9 is bigger!
15 is further along when you count — it's bigger!
20 is two groups of 10 — that's more than one group of 10!
7 is further when you count up — 7 is bigger than 2!
18 is closer to 20, so 18 is the bigger number!

question: Which one does NOT belong?
hint: Three are fruits and one is not!

question: Which one does NOT belong?
hint: Three are animals and one is a plant!

question: Which one does NOT belong?
hint: Three numbers are even (skip counting by 2) and one is odd!

question: Which one does NOT belong?
hint: Three are circles and one is a star shape!

question: Which one does NOT belong?
hint: Three are odd numbers and one is even!

question: Which one does NOT belong?
hint: Three travel on the ground and one flies in the air!

question: Which group has MORE?
hint: Count each group — 5 is more than 3!

question: Which group has FEWER?
hint: 2 stars is fewer than 5 stars!

question: Which group has MORE?
hint: Count them up — 6 is more than 3!

question: Which group has FEWER?
hint: 2 bananas is fewer than 4 bananas!

question: Which group has MORE?
hint: Count them — 7 stars is more than 4 stars!

question: Which group has FEWER?
hint: Just 1 frog is fewer than 5 frogs!

question: Which is LONGER?
hint: A snake stretches out very long!

question: Which is TALLER?
hint: A giraffe has a super long neck — it's the tallest animal!

question: Which is HEAVIER?
hint: An elephant is huge and heavy — way heavier than a tiny mouse!

question: Which is SHORTER?
hint: A house is shorter than a big castle!

question: Which is LIGHTER?
hint: A feather floats in the air — it's super light!

question: Which is BIGGER?
hint: A whale is the biggest animal in the ocean!

question: Which is HEAVIER?
hint: A car is made of metal — way heavier than a soft teddy bear!

question: Which is TALLER?
hint: Sunflowers grow really tall — taller than tulips!

question: The cat is ___ the house.
hint: Look! The cat is right beside the house!

question: The rain is ___ the house.
hint: Rain falls from the sky — it's up above the house!

question: The dog is ___ the box.
hint: The dog is sitting up high on the box!

question: The cat is ___ the box.
hint: The cat is hiding down below the box!

question: The box is ___ the two frogs.
hint: The box has a frog on each side — it's in the middle!

question: The present is ___ the tree.
hint: Presents go under the Christmas tree!

question: When do you eat breakfast?
hint: We eat breakfast when we first wake up — in the morning!

question: When do you see the moon?
hint: The moon comes out when it gets dark — at night!

question: When do you brush your teeth before sleeping?
hint: Before you go to bed — that's nighttime!

question: When does the sun rise?
hint: The sun comes up to start the day — in the morning!

question: When do you eat lunch at school?
hint: Lunch is in the middle of the day — the afternoon!

question: Which coin is worth MORE?
hint: A dime is 10 cents — that's the most!

question: Which coin is worth MORE?
hint: A quarter is 25 cents — that's the most!

question: Which coin is worth the LEAST?
hint: A penny is just 1 cent — the smallest amount!

question: How many pennies make a nickel?
hint: A nickel is 5 cents, and each penny is 1 cent, so you need 5 pennies!

question: You have a quarter (25¢). Can you buy a toy that costs 20¢?
hint: 25 cents is more than 20 cents — you have enough!
